articles.title: ECB's Kocher warns June rate hike unavoidable if Hormuz stays shut
articles.expand: ECB Governing Council member Kocher says a June rate hike is unavoidable if the Hormuz Strait remains closed, warning prolonged conflict will push eurozone inflation materially higher. articles.title: Dollar Rallies and Gold Falls as T-note Yields Climb
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Tuesday rallied to a 6-week high, finishing up by 0.14%. The dollar moved higher on Tuesday as higher T-note yields are strengthening the dollar’s interest rate differentials. The 10-year T-note yield jumped to a 16-month high on Tuesday of 4.685%. articles.expand: Two sessions in, WTI is holding above $103, the 10-year yield has surged to 4.70%, and Wednesday’s FOMC minutes are now the week’s most important market catalyst.

articles.title: Financial & Forex Market Recap – May 19, 2026
articles.expand: Treasury yields hit multi-year highs and the U.S. dollar strengthened as Iran war inflation fears kept Fed rate hike speculation alive, while gold and equities fell sharply.

articles.expand: The WSJ is reporting: Iran’s negotiating position remains largely unchanged, with mediators and U.S. officials saying there has been little progress toward a breakthrough deal. articles.expand: The AUDUSD extended lower on Monday, continuing the downside momentum that began late last week on Thursday and Friday. However, as the pair pushed lower, buyers started to lean against a key swing area between 0.7100 and 0.7113, helping to slow the decline and stabilize the price action in early trading. That support zone once again proved to be a...

articles.title: UK Jobs Market Takes a Hit as Iran War Energy Shock Bites
articles.expand: UK payrolled employees fell 100,000 in April — the biggest monthly drop since COVID — as the Iran war energy shock hammers businesses and sends the unemployment rate rising.

articles.title: XAG/USD Price Forecast: Silver struggles below key moving averages amid hawkish Fed bets
articles.expand: Silver (XAG/USD) remains under pressure on Tuesday as a stronger US Dollar (USD) and rising US Treasury yields continue to weigh on the precious metal amid growing expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) may need to raise interest rates to tackle rising Oil-driven inflation pressures.

articles.title: BoJ Governor Ueda: Aware that long-term interest rates are rising rapidly
articles.expand: Bank of Japan (BoJ) Governor Kazuo Ueda said that they need to closely monitor signs of upward price pressure and that he will take appropriate monetary policy to achieve the BoJ inflation target, after a G7 finance chiefs and central bankers' meeting in Paris on Tuesday.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Correction has further legs to go
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar’s uptrend seems to have met some decent resistance in the 0.7270-0.7280 band so far, with AUD/USD still looking for a strong catalyst to extend its rally and dispute the yearly peaks and higher levels.

articles.title: XAG/USD Price analysis: Silver testing critical support, $61 next?
articles.expand: Market participants maintain a focus on war developments. Continued tensions between the United States (US) and Iran put financial boards in risk-off mode on Tuesday. The US Dollar (USD) is the overall winner, appreciating against all major rivals.

articles.title: US Dollar Index hits more than one-month highs on Iran tensions and hawkish Fed bets
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the Greenback's value against a basket of six major currencies, extends its rally on Tuesday, climbing near more than one-month highs as stalled US-Iran negotiations and hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) expectations support the US Dollar.

articles.title: Canada: Energy-driven CPI rise supports BoC hold – RBC
articles.expand: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) economist Abbey Xu notes that Canadian inflation accelerated in April, mainly due to higher energy prices and fading base effects, while underlying pressures stayed contained.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: Soft CPI delays recovery – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ FX strategists Howard Du and Linda Cheng note that soft April Canada inflation and weak employment data should keep USD/CAD supported near 1.37 in Q2 2026.

articles.title: The Warsh Trade and the US Dollar – EUR/USD, GBP/USD & Dollar Index (DXY) overview
articles.expand: Forex Market update: The US Dollar rallies sharply against major currencies ahead of newly confirmed Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's upcoming swearing-in ceremony. Driven by widening yield gaps, sticky inflation, and expectations of a leaner central bank balance sheet, explore critical technical targets for the DXY, EUR/USD, and GBP/USD.

articles.title: BoE: Inflation scenarios hinge on oil price path – DBS
articles.expand: Philip Wee of DBS Group Research explains how Oil prices shape the Bank of England’s (BoE) policy outlook. The baseline assumes Oil around USD108 supports manageable second-round inflation effects and may require one or two rate hikes.

articles.title: Why the Pound is starting to trade like an emerging-market currency
articles.expand: The Bank of England (BoE) is being priced to hike around 70 basis points before year-end. The 10-year gilt is near 5.2%, its highest since the 2008 financial crisis. The 30-year sits at levels last seen in 1998. None of it is helping the Pound.
